specific_location: Below Greenhorn Creek Diversion Gate south of Milepost 32, CSH 165.
description: This bird was nearly robin sized with slightly shorter tail. It had a slaty gray back and fairly bright orange underparts with dusky grayish speckles on lower belly. A complete slate gray breast band crossed the orange breast and was slightly darker than gray back. Throat was orange. Face,nape,and crown were slate gray with orange supercilium.   Wings were a mixture of gray and orange with orangish wing bars, tertials, various orange & gray coverts, and blackish primary tips.  Bill and eye were dark.Behaviors: Foraged in shallow slow moving creek.Call: None givenPlumage: adult
